titleStmt.title: SII 19.165: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao, Dines Chandra Sircar and G.S. Gai
No. 165. (A.R. No. 319 of 1910.) TIRUMEYJÑĀNAM, KUMBHAKONAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT. ON THE WEST WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, JÑĀNAPARAMĒŚVARA TEMPLE.

summary.p: This registers a sale of land belonging to the mahāsabhā including the Nambimār of Nālūr a brahmadēya village of Śēṟṟūr-kūṟṟam, to the temple of Tirukkaṟkuḍīśvarattu-Perumāṉ (evidently the same as Jñānaparamēśvara), in the village, for meeting the requirements of rice offerings to the god during the three services in the temple, with 4 nāḻi of rice for each service during which a light was also to be burnt. Among the boundaries of the land is mentioned a land belonging to the Mahāmātras of the village.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . kop
rakecari panmaṟkki yā
ṇṭu 6- Āṟāvatu ce
ṟṟūrkkūṟṟattu bra
hmateyam nālūr na
mpimāruḷḷiṭṭa mahā
sabhaip peruṅkuṟi
p perumakkaḷom
Ivvūrttirukkaṟku
ṭiśvarattup perumā
ṉ ṟaṇṭeśvararkkuc
cantirādittavaṟ Iṟai
yiliyāka yāṅkaḷ vi
ṟṟukkuṭutta ṉilamāva
tu Ivvūrp pakanvāyi
ṉ vaṭakkil ṉilam sabhai
yomitāy sabhaino
kki kiṭanta nilattukkuk
kiḻpāṟkellai vāy
kkālukku me
ṟkkun teṉpāṟ
kkellai yiv
vūr mahaāmāttirar
kaḷ ṉilattukku vaṭak
ku melpāṟkkellai
y māṭalaṉ pāṇ
ṭaṉ ṟattaṉun tampi
mārum nilattukku tiru
ccittirakūṭattu de
var nilattukkuṅ ki
ḻakkum vaṭapāṟkel
lai madhyastha
ṉ kāṭappuli
kāṟāyilaṭika
ḷ nilattukku
māṭalaṉ tā
motiraṉāra
ṇaṉ makkaḷ
nilattukku
n teṟkkum
Ivvicait
ta Iṉāṉ
kellaiyi
l catukkunir
pāyappe
ṟuvatākavum
viṟṟukkuṭut
tom mahā
sabhaiyom I
nnilaṅ koṇ
ṭu muṉṟu sandhiyum
potu ṉā(ṉ)ṉāḻi
Araci koṇṭu tiruva
mirtu kāṭṭuvatākavu
m kaṟiamitukku mitu
veyāvutākavum mu
ṉṟu sandhi poto
ru viḷakku
Erikkakaṭa
vatākavum
Itu panmā
heśva
rarakṣai .
Digital edition of SII 19.165 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
